creator: alane@geeksrus.net
text:
I did a lot of work building CVS rpms for RH7.1 up until I switched to 
FreeBSD earlier this month. The KDE2.2 code is *old*. This was a recurring 
problem on Linux as well. Try the new ports (2.21). kdelibs2 has an error in 
the dependencies (libxml2.4.6) but it'll build and install (with -f) just 
fine. Then build kdebase2. I'm submitting a patch later today for its packing 
list file, but basically just cut out the share/config/kdm/X* files. 

IIRC A KDE 2.2.2 port update is coming in a couple of weeks, which should 
bring the code up to sync with CVS (more or less) again. I'd not waste too 
much time on things until that happens. KDE2 was a very fast moving target, 
and even after release interfaces kept changing (although infrequently) as 
code was fixed and reworked.
